summary: The group's rank outsiders — safety-first and defensively excellent, but chronically blunt in attack. Conceded zero goals across 10 qualifiers, yet that attacking bluntness cost Sami Trabelsi his job after a limp AFCON exit to Mali; ex-Nottingham Forest boss Sabri Lamouchi (1-0 vs Haiti, 0-0 vs Canada) took over and is no more adventurous.
style: A disciplined, stripped-back defensive 4-5-1 block — long a cornerstone of the Tunisian identity. Tough to break down, organised, and patient, but with little attacking thrust; they'll likely need set pieces and a slice of luck to create.

counterReliance.note: Defend deep in a 4-5-1; limited attacking output

strengths: Elite defensive solidity — no goals conceded in 10 qualifiers
Strength in midfield duels to soak up long spells without the ball
weaknesses: Chronic attacking bluntness — the issue that got the previous coach sacked
Reliant on set pieces and luck for goals; never reached the knockouts in five prior World Cups

keyPlayers.name: Rani Khedira
keyPlayers.note: Brother of 2014 World Cup winner Sami; switched allegiance to Tunisia in 2026 — his duel strength is vital for a side that defends for long stretches

keyPlayers.name: Hannibal Mejbri
keyPlayers.note: The creative spark — a dribbler who can make something from little when chances are scarce

tactics: Sit in a disciplined 4-5-1, stay compact, and frustrate — then hope a set piece or moment of individual quality unlocks a goal. Defensively they'll trouble anyone; the question is whether they can score enough to escape the group.
notes: Five previous World Cup appearances, never past the group stage.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group F (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
